lead.name: claude
output:
```python
"""Quick and dirty auth module - NOT FOR PRODUCTION USE"""

import hashlib
import secrets
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

# In-memory storage (obviously terrible for production)
_users: dict[str, "User"] = {}
_sessions: dict[str, str] = {}  # token -> email
_reset_tokens: dict[str, tuple[str, float]] = {}  # token -> (email, expiry)


@dataclass
class User:
    email: str
    password_hash: str
    salt: str
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)


def _hash_password(password: str, salt: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(f"{password}{salt}".encode()).hexdigest()


def register(email: str, password: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Register a new user. Returns (success, message)."""
    email = email.lower().strip()
    
    if not email or "@" not in email:
        return False, "Invalid email"
    
    if len(password) < 4:
        return False, "Password too short"
    
    if email in _users:
        return False, "User already exists"
    
    salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
    password_hash = _hash_password(password, salt)
    _users[email] = User(email=email, password_hash=password_hash, salt=salt)
    
    return True, "Registration successful"


def login(email: str, password: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Login user. Returns (success, token_or_error)."""
    email = email.lower().strip()
    
    user = _users.get(email)
    if not user:
        return False, "Invalid credentials"
    
    if _hash_password(password, user.salt) != user.password_hash:
        return False, "Invalid credentials"
    
    token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
    _sessions[token] = email
    
    return True, token


def logout(token: str) -> bool:
    """Logout user by invalidating token."""
    return _sessions.pop(token, None) is not None


def get_current_user(token: str) -> Optional[str]:
    """Get email of logged in user from token."""
    return _sessions.get(token)


def request_password_reset(email: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Request password reset. Returns (success, token_or_message)."""
    email = email.lower().strip()
    
    if email not in _users:
        # Don't reveal if user exists
        return True, "If account exists, reset link sent"
    
    token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
    expiry = time.time() + 3600  # 1 hour
    _reset_tokens[token] = (email, expiry)
    
    # In real app, send email here
    return True, token  # Returning token directly for demo purposes


def reset_password(token: str, new_password: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Reset password using token. Returns (success, message)."""
    if token not in _reset_tokens:
        return False, "Invalid or expired token"
    
    email, expiry = _reset_tokens[token]
    
    if time.time() > expiry:
        del _reset_tokens[token]
        return False, "Token expired"
    
    if len(new_password) < 4:
        return False, "Password too short"
    
    user = _users.get(email)
    if not user:
        return False, "User not found"
    
    salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
    user.salt = salt
    user.password_hash = _hash_password(new_password, salt)
    
    del _reset_tokens[token]
    # Invalidate all sessions for this user
    _sessions = {k: v for k, v in _sessions.items() if v != email}
    
    return True, "Password reset successful"


def change_password(token: str, old_password: str, new_password: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Change password for logged in user."""
    email = get_current_user(token)
    if not email:
        return False, "Not logged in"
    
    user = _users[email]
    if _hash_password(old_password, user.salt) != user.password_hash:
        return False, "Invalid old password"
    
    if len(new_password) < 4:
        return False, "Password too short"
    
    salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
    user.salt = salt
    user.password_hash = _hash_password(new_password, salt)
    
    return True, "Password changed"


# Quick test
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Register
    ok, msg = register("test@example.com", "pass123")
    print(f"Register: {ok}, {msg}")
    
    # Login
    ok, token = login("test@example.com", "pass123")
    print(f"Login: {ok}, token={token[:20]}...")
    
    # Check session
    user = get_current_user(token)
    print(f"Current user: {user}")
    
    # Password reset flow
    ok, reset_token = request_password_reset("test@example.com")
    print(f"Reset request: {ok}")
    
    ok, msg = reset_password(reset_token, "newpass456")
    print(f"Reset: {ok}, {msg}")
    
    # Old password should fail
    ok, msg = login("test@example.com", "pass123")
    print(f"Old password login: {ok}, {msg}")
    
    # New password should work
    ok, token = login("test@example.com", "newpass456")
    print(f"New password login: {ok}")
```

finding.name: gemini
output: Uses weak SHA-256 hashing; use a slow algorithm like Argon2 or bcrypt to prevent brute-force attacks.

fix.name: gemini
output:
```python
import bcrypt

def _hash_password(password: str, salt: str) -> str:
    # The 'salt' parameter from the original function signature is not directly
    # used for concatenation with the password in bcrypt, as bcrypt handles
    # salting internally using bcrypt.gensalt(). The resulting hash string
    # will contain its own securely generated salt.
    hashed_password = bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode('utf-8'), bcrypt.gensalt())
    return hashed_password.decode('utf-8')
```

```python
"""Quick and dirty auth module - NOT FOR PRODUCTION USE"""

import bcrypt
import secrets
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

# In-memory storage (obviously terrible for production)
_users: dict[str, "User"] = {}
_sessions: dict[str, str] = {}  # token -> email
_reset_tokens: dict[str, tuple[str, float]] = {}  # token -> (email, expiry)


@dataclass
class User:
    email: str
    password_hash: str
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)


def _hash_password(password: str) -> str:
    hashed_password = bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode('utf-8'), bcrypt.gensalt())
    return hashed_password.decode('utf-8')


def _verify_password(password: str, password_hash: str) -> bool:
    return bcrypt.checkpw(password.encode('utf-8'), password_hash.encode('utf-8'))


def register(email: str, password: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Register a new user. Returns (success, message)."""
    email = email.lower().strip()
    
    if not email or "@" not in email:
        return False, "Invalid email"
    
    if len(password) < 4:
        return False, "Password too short"
    
    if email in _users:
        return False, "User already exists"
    
    password_hash = _hash_password(password)
    _users[email] = User(email=email, password_hash=password_hash)
    
    return True, "Registration successful"


def login(email: str, password: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Login user. Returns (success, token_or_error)."""
    email = email.lower().strip()
    
    user = _users.get(email)
    if not user:
        return False, "Invalid credentials"
    
    if not _verify_password(password, user.password_hash):
        return False, "Invalid credentials"
    
    token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
    _sessions[token] = email
    
    return True, token


def logout(token: str) -> bool:
    """Logout user by invalidating token."""
    return _sessions.pop(token, None) is not None


def get_current_user(token: str) -> Optional[str]:
    """Get email of logged in user from token."""
    return _sessions.get(token)


def request_password_reset(email: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Request password reset. Returns (success, token_or_message)."""
    email = email.lower().strip()
    
    if email not in _users:
        # Don't reveal if user exists
        return True, "If account exists, reset link sent"
    
    token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
    expiry = time.time() + 3600  # 1 hour
    _reset_tokens[token] = (email, expiry)
    
    # In real app, send email here
    return True, token  # Returning token directly for demo purposes


def reset_password(token: str, new_password: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Reset password using token. Returns (success, message)."""
    global _sessions
    
    if token not in _reset_tokens:
        return False, "Invalid or expired token"
    
    email, expiry = _reset_tokens[token]
    
    if time.time() > expiry:
        del _reset_tokens[token]
        return False, "Token expired"
    
    if len(new_password) < 4:
        return False, "Password too short"
    
    user = _users.get(email)
    if not user:
        return False, "User not found"
    
    user.password_hash = _hash_password(new_password)
    
    del _reset_tokens[token]
    # Invalidate all sessions for this user
    _sessions = {k: v for k, v in _sessions.items() if v != email}
    
    return True, "Password reset successful"


def change_password(token: str, old_password: str, new_password: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Change password for logged in user."""
    email = get_current_user(token)
    if not email:
        return False, "Not logged in"
    
    user = _users[email]
    if not _verify_password(old_password, user.password_hash):
        return False, "Invalid old password"
    
    if len(new_password) < 4:
        return False, "Password too short"
    
    user.password_hash = _hash_password(new_password)
    
    return True, "Password changed"


# Quick test
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Register
    ok, msg = register("test@example.com", "pass123")
    print(f"Register: {ok}, {msg}")
    
    # Login
    ok, token = login("test@example.com", "pass123")
    print(f"Login: {ok}, token={token[:20]}...")
    
    # Check session
    user = get_current_user(token)
    print(f"Current user: {user}")
    
    # Password reset flow
    ok, reset_token = request_password_reset("test@example.com")
    print(f"Reset request: {ok}")
    
    ok, msg = reset_password(reset_token, "newpass456")
    print(f"Reset: {ok}, {msg}")
    
    # Old password should fail
    ok, msg = login("test@example.com", "pass123")
    print(f"Old password login: {ok}, {msg}")
    
    # New password should work
    ok, token = login("test@example.com", "newpass456")
    print(f"New password login: {ok}")
```
